evals.description: Trigger test: activates on IAM design question
evals.prompt: I need to give my Lambda function access to read from an S3 bucket and call a DynamoDB table. How should I set up the IAM permissions?

evals.description: Trigger test: activates on secrets management question
evals.prompt: We have database credentials that our app needs to connect to RDS. Right now they're in an environment variable in our ECS task definition. Is this OK?

evals.description: Trigger test: activates on VPC security question
evals.prompt: I'm setting up a new AWS environment for a production web app. How should I structure the VPC and subnets?

evals.description: Core task: create a minimal IAM policy for S3 read access
evals.prompt: Write an IAM policy that allows a service to only read objects from a specific S3 bucket named 'company-reports' and nothing else.

evals.description: Core task: explain the envelope encryption pattern
evals.prompt: Our compliance team is asking us to use customer-managed KMS keys for our RDS database. Can you explain envelope encryption and why it matters here?

evals.description: Core task: configure CloudTrail for organization-wide audit logging
evals.prompt: We have 12 AWS accounts in an organization and need to ensure every account's API calls are logged in a tamper-proof way for compliance. How do I set this up?

evals.description: Core task: implement mTLS between services with Istio
evals.prompt: We're running microservices on Kubernetes and our security team wants all service-to-service traffic to be mutually authenticated and encrypted. How do we implement this with Istio?
We're starting our SOC 2 Type II audit prep. What are the key technical controls we need to have in place for our AWS infrastructure?

evals.description: Gotcha: warns against wildcard IAM policies
evals.prompt: To keep things simple, we're just giving our application's IAM role the managed policy 'AdministratorAccess'. Is there a reason not to do this?

evals.description: Gotcha: warns against hardcoding secrets in source code
evals.prompt: I just committed our AWS access key and secret to the GitHub repo by mistake. It was only there for 30 seconds before I deleted the commit. Am I safe?

evals.description: Gotcha: warns against placing databases in public subnets
evals.prompt: We're deploying RDS in the same public subnet as our load balancer to simplify the network setup. What's wrong with this approach?

evals.description: References load: points to compliance frameworks reference for HIPAA question
evals.prompt: We're building a healthcare app and need to understand what AWS controls are required for HIPAA compliance. Where should I start?
